children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish an alternative trade adjustment assistance program for older workers that provides the benefits described in paragraph (2).August 6, 2002

children.label: i
children.text: the wages received by the worker from reemployment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the wages received by the worker at the time of separation.

children.heading: Health insurance
children.label: B Health insurance
children.text: A worker described in paragraph (3)(B) participating in the program established under paragraph (1) is eligible to receive, for a period not to exceed 2 years, a credit for health insurance costs under , as added by section 201 of the Trade Act of 2002.section 35 of title 26

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the opportunity for a group of workers on whose behalf a petition is filed under to request that the group of workers be certified for the alternative trade adjustment assistance program under this section at the time the petition is filed.section 2271 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: Whether a significant number of workers in the workers’ firm are 50 years of age or older.

children.label: II
children.text: Whether the workers in the workers’ firm possess skills that are not easily transferable.

children.label: III
children.text: The competitive conditions within the workers’ industry.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: iii Deadline
children.text: The Secretary shall determine whether the workers in the group are eligible for the alternative trade adjustment assistance program by the date specified in .section 2273(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: is covered by a certification under subpart A of this part;

children.label: ii
children.text: obtains reemployment not more than 26 weeks after the date of separation from the adversely affected employment;

children.label: iii
children.text: is at least 50 years of age;

children.label: iv
children.text: earns not more than $50,000 a year in wages from reemployment;

children.label: v
children.text: is employed on a full-time basis as defined by State law in the State in which the worker is employed; and

children.label: vi
children.text: does not return to the employment from which the worker was separated.

children.heading: Total amount of payments
children.label: 4 Total amount of payments
children.text: The payments described in paragraph (2)(A) made to a worker may not exceed $10,000 per worker during the 2-year eligibility period.

children.heading: Limitation on other benefits
children.label: 5 Limitation on other benefits
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2)(B), if a worker is receiving payments pursuant to the program established under paragraph (1), the worker shall not be eligible to receive any other benefits under this subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), no payments may be made by a State under the program established under subsection (a)(1) after the date that is 5 years after the date on which such program is implemented by the State.1See Application of Subsection (b)(1) note below.1

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), a worker receiving payments under the program established under subsection (a)(1) on the termination date described in paragraph (1) shall continue to receive such payments if the worker meets the criteria described in subsection (a)(3)(B).
